name: Goodfellow et al., 'Generative Adversarial Nets', NeurIPS 2014

task.description: Create a self-contained 1920x1080 HTML slide titled 'Generative Adversarial Nets (Goodfellow et al., 2014)'. LEFT half (50%): an inline-SVG diagram - a noise box 'z ~ p_z' feeds Generator 'G' producing 'fake x'; real data 'x ~ p_data' and 'fake x' both feed Discriminator 'D' which outputs 'real / fake'; a dashed arrow shows the gradient feeding back to G. RIGHT half (50%), three stacked equal-width full-width cards sharing the same left and right edges with uniform vertical gaps: card 1 the value function 'min_G max_D V(D,G) = E_{x~p_data}[log D(x)] + E_{z~p_z}[log(1 - D(G(z)))]' with real subscripts/superscripts; card 2 a 4-line algorithm box ('for number of training iterations:', '  sample minibatch of z and x', '  update D by ascending its gradient', '  update G by descending its gradient'); card 3 two bullets ('At optimum p_g = p_data, D = 1/2', 'No explicit density needed'). Footnote at the bottom. ALSO add a fourth right-hand card with a 3x3 grid of generated-sample placeholders (equal-size cells) and a metrics line 'Inception Score / FID'.
requirements: No element overlaps any other; the feedback/gradient arrow does not pass through G's or D's label
The value-function equation renders subscripts (p_data, p_z) and is fully inside its card, not clipped
The three right-hand cards share the same left edge and the same right edge (equal width, vertically aligned)
The three right-hand cards have uniform vertical gaps between them
The algorithm box keeps its line indentation and all four lines are fully visible
The left diagram (z, G, x, D, outputs) is fully inside the left half with nothing crossing the centre line
Nothing overflows the 1920x1080 canvas; no scrollbar
The footnote is fully visible above the bottom edge and overlaps nothing
Both bullets in the third card are at most two lines and fully inside the card
Every text element is at least 12px
The fourth card's 3x3 sample grid has 9 equal-size aligned cells and does not overlap the other cards
task.paper: Goodfellow et al., 'Generative Adversarial Nets', NeurIPS 2014

ss_html:
Generative Adversarial Nets (Goodfellow et al., 2014)

Value Function

minG maxD V(D,G) = Ex~pdata[log D(x)] + Ez~pz[log(1 - D(G(z)))]

Training Algorithm

for number of training iterations:
sample minibatch of z and x
update D by ascending its gradient
update G by descending its gradient

Key Properties

  • At optimum pg = pdata, D = 1/2
  • No explicit density needed

Generated Samples

sample
sample
sample
sample
sample
sample
sample
sample
sample
Inception Score / FID
Goodfellow, I., Pouget-Abadie, J., Mirza, M., Xu, B., Warde-Farley, D., Ozair, S., ... & Bengio, Y. (2014). Generative adversarial nets. NIPS.
rv_html:
Generative Adversarial Nets (Goodfellow et al., 2014)

Value Function

minG maxD V(D,G) = Ex~pdata[log D(x)] + Ez~pz[log(1 - D(G(z)))]

Training Algorithm

for number of training iterations:
sample minibatch of z and x
update D by ascending its gradient
update G by descending its gradient

Key Properties

  • At optimum pg = pdata, D = 1/2
  • No explicit density needed

Generated Samples

training framework for generative modeling

Writing SVG code... ```html

  2. [MAJOR] The three right-hand cards do not have equal width - the bottom card appears wider than the top two cards (at right)
  3. [MAJOR] The vertical gaps between the three right-hand cards are not uniform - the gap between cards 2 and 3 appears larger than the gap between cards 1 and 2 (at right)
  4. [MAJOR] The sample grid in the bottom right card only shows 4 visible cells instead of the required 9 equal-size aligned cells in a 3x3 grid (at bottom-right)

Suggested fixes:
  1. Reroute the gradient feedback arrow to avoid passing through the Generator 'G' label
  2. Ensure all three right-hand cards have identical width and are properly aligned
  3. Standardize the vertical spacing between the three right-hand cards
  4. Complete the 3x3 sample grid with 9 properly sized and aligned cells
